_meta.description: Mapping between Stanford ContractNLI hypotheses (English, NDA-focused) and Justitia AR hypotheses (Argentine law, CCyC-grounded)
_meta.ccyc: Ley 26.994 — Código Civil y Comercial de la Nación (Argentina, 2015)

mapping.nli_hypothesis: All Confidential Information shall be expressly identified by the Disclosing Party.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: La información confidencial se identifica de forma expresa y taxativa en el contrato.
mapping.adaptation_note: Direct equivalent. Argentine practice often uses broad catch-all definitions; expressly-identified-only clauses are less common.

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Confidential Information may include verbally conveyed information.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: La información confidencial incluye información transmitida en forma verbal u oral.
mapping.adaptation_note: Direct equivalent. Under CCyC verbal agreements are generally valid (Art. 284); including verbal CI is common in Argentine NDAs.

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Receiving Party shall not disclose the fact that negotiations take place.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: Las obligaciones de confidencialidad son recíprocas entre las partes.
mapping.adaptation_note: Partial mapping. ContractNLI nda-3 covers non-disclosure of negotiations existence; AR-03 captures reciprocity, which is a more commercially relevant distinction in the Argentine market.

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Purpose.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: El uso de la información confidencial está limitado exclusivamente al propósito u objeto definido en el contrato.
mapping.ar_legal_basis: Arts. 1061-1065 CCyC (interpretación del contrato); Art. 279 CCyC (objeto)

mapping.ar_hypothesis: El contrato no otorga licencias sobre derechos de propiedad intelectual de ninguna de las partes.
mapping.adaptation_note: No direct ContractNLI equivalent. Critical for Argentine tech and startup contracts where IP ownership is frequently disputed.

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Receiving Party shall protect the received Confidential Information by employing the same means it uses to protect its own Confidential Information.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: El receptor protege la información confidencial con el mismo grado de diligencia que aplica a su propia información confidencial de igual naturaleza.
mapping.ar_legal_basis: Art. 1724-1725 CCyC (culpa y dolo); Art. 1723 CCyC (responsabilidad objetiva)
mapping.adaptation_note: Direct equivalent. Argentine law distinguishes culpa leve/grave/dolo — the 'same means' standard maps to culpa leve.

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Receiving Party may share some Confidential Information with some of Receiving Party's employees.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: El receptor puede compartir información confidencial con sus propios empleados o colaboradores, bajo obligación de confidencialidad equivalente.
mapping.ar_legal_basis: Art. 85 LCT (deber de fidelidad del trabajador); Art. 1021 CCyC
mapping.adaptation_note: Direct equivalent. Note that under Argentine labor law (LCT), employees already have a statutory confidentiality duty (Art. 85 LCT).

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Receiving Party may share some Confidential Information with third-parties (including consultants, agents and professional advisors).
mapping.ar_hypothesis: El receptor puede compartir información confidencial con asesores externos, consultores o terceros autorizados, bajo reserva.
mapping.ar_legal_basis: Arts. 1021-1022 CCyC (efectos respecto de terceros); Art. 1326 CCyC (agencia)

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Receiving Party shall not reverse engineer any objects which embody Disclosing Party's Confidential Information.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: El contrato prohíbe la ingeniería inversa de productos o sistemas que contengan información confidencial.
mapping.adaptation_note: Direct equivalent. Argentine IP law does not expressly permit reverse engineering for interoperability (unlike EU Directive 2009/24/EC), making this clause broadly enforceable.

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Receiving Party shall not engage in activities that are competitive with the Disclosing Party.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: El contrato establece obligaciones de no competencia o exclusividad para el receptor.
mapping.adaptation_note: Direct equivalent with critical caveat: Argentine courts scrutinize non-compete clauses under Art. 988 CCyC. Time-unlimited or overly broad clauses risk nullity. Flag duration and geographic scope.

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Receiving Party shall notify Disclosing Party in case Receiving Party is required by law, regulation or judicial order to disclose Confidential Information.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: El receptor debe notificar al divulgante si es requerido por autoridad pública, judicial o regulatoria a revelar información confidencial.
mapping.ar_legal_basis: Art. 992 CCyC; BCRA Com. A (para entidades financieras); RG CNV (para mercado de capitales)
mapping.adaptation_note: Direct equivalent. Add regulatory overlay: BCRA, CNV, AFIP, ANMAT requirements may impose their own disclosure timelines that override contractual notice periods.

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Some obligations of Agreement may survive termination.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: El contrato establece un plazo determinado para las obligaciones de confidencialidad y/o estas sobreviven a la extinción.
mapping.ar_legal_basis: Art. 1078 CCyC (efectos de la extinción); Art. 1545 CCyC (plazo)
mapping.adaptation_note: ContractNLI nda-15 covers survival generally; Justitia splits this into AR-12 (fixed term) and AR-13 (post-termination survival) because Argentine courts treat them differently for enforceability.

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Receiving Party shall destroy or return some Confidential Information upon the termination of Agreement.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: El receptor debe devolver o destruir la información confidencial y sus copias al vencimiento o extinción del contrato.
mapping.ar_legal_basis: Art. 1200 CCyC (extinción de obligaciones); Ley 25.326 Art. 12 (destrucción de datos)
mapping.adaptation_note: Direct equivalent. For digital information, include reference to Ley 25.326 (data destruction obligations).

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Receiving Party may develop information similar to Confidential Information.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: El contrato prevé excepciones a la confidencialidad para información de dominio público, conocimiento previo independiente o desarrollo autónomo.
mapping.adaptation_note: Partial mapping. Justitia AR-15 groups all standard carve-outs (public domain, prior knowledge, independent development) which are implied by Argentine law even without express clause.

mapping.nli_hypothesis: Residual allows for Receiving Party's employees to retain some Confidential Information.
mapping.ar_hypothesis: El contrato incluye una cláusula de 'residuales': información retenida en memoria sin esfuerzo deliberado queda excluida de las obligaciones.
mapping.adaptation_note: Residual clauses originate in US/UK practice. No Argentine court has ruled on their enforceability under CCyC. Flag their presence as a notable deviation from standard Argentine practice.

mapping.ar_hypothesis: El contrato establece jurisdicción argentina y ley aplicable argentina como derecho rector.
mapping.ar_legal_basis: Arts. 2650-2651 CCyC (DIPr — autonomía de la voluntad)
mapping.adaptation_note: No ContractNLI equivalent (dataset uses US NDAs). Critical for Argentine practice: many contracts with foreign counterparties omit this or choose foreign law, affecting enforceability of Argentine mandatory rules.

mapping.ar_hypothesis: El contrato prevé un mecanismo de resolución alternativa de disputas.
mapping.adaptation_note: No ContractNLI equivalent. In Argentina, civil/commercial mediation is mandatory prior to litigation in most jurisdictions (Ley 26.589). Arbitration clauses must comply with Ley 27.449 for international contracts.

mapping.ar_hypothesis: El contrato establece la titularidad de toda propiedad intelectual desarrollada durante la vigencia de la relación contractual.
mapping.ar_legal_basis: Ley 11.723 Arts. 4-6 (titularidad); Art. 82-83 LCT (invenciones del trabajador)
mapping.adaptation_note: No ContractNLI equivalent. Highly material in Argentine tech contracts: under Ley 11.723, the default rule favors the creator. Without express assignment, IP stays with the developer/employee.

mapping.ar_hypothesis: El contrato regula el tratamiento de datos personales conforme a la legislación argentina vigente.
mapping.ar_legal_basis: Ley 25.326 (PDPA); Disposiciones AAIP; nuevo proyecto de ley de protección de datos (2024)
mapping.adaptation_note: No ContractNLI equivalent. Mandatory for contracts involving personal data processing. Argentine PDPA (Ley 25.326) requires data transfer agreements and is currently being updated to align with GDPR-level standards.

coverage_summary.notes: nda-5 (non-solicitation) and nda-6 (residual clause variation) were not mapped as they address concepts covered by Argentine labor law (LCT) rather than contractual clauses.
